findings.diabetes_subtype: Type 2 — early stage, subclinical insulin resistance

primary_medications.name: Metformin
primary_medications.rationale: First-line preventive therapy — early intervention while clinical markers are still normal

findings.monitoring_plan: HbA1c every 6 months, fasting glucose quarterly, repeat transcriptomics at 6 months to track pathway progression
findings.medication_summary: Metformin monotherapy (conservative). Early intervention approach given subclinical molecular findings with normal clinical presentation.

summary: Recommended conservative monotherapy: Metformin 500-1000mg/day for early T2D intervention. Molecular evidence supports treatment despite normal clinical labs. Monitor pathway progression at 6 months.
